positiveClaims: The explicit owner-selected release claim command execution witness path is product-bound as bounded internal Review Bridge 12M command execution witness-only evidence.
Accepted 12M requires the command id cmd.project.releaseClaim.execute to be present in the command surface kernel allowlist.
Accepted 12M requires bus-route command surface dispatch; direct route bypass remains blocked.
Accepted 12M calls the already-bound 12K execution gate and wraps the upstream accepted, diagnostics, or blocked output into a typed witness envelope.
Accepted 12M rejects non-plain or inherited top-level payload objects before invoking the 12K execution gate.
Accepted 12M preserves typed witness envelope results only for the bounded release-claim command surface ids without widening typed-result passthrough to unrelated command ids.

At the real ui:command-bridge boundary, accepted 12M returns as outer ok true value, while diagnostics or blocked witness output remains preserved under the outer command bridge failure envelope value field.
Accepted 12M emits deterministic non-mutating command execution witness-only output and keeps witnessOnly true.

This contour is command execution witness-only evidence; it is not command availability, command execution, release execution completion, product publication, release readiness, import, export, apply, storage truth, or project truth.
nonClaims: No release execution completion is claimed.
No release publication completion is claimed.
No release-wide publication authority is claimed.
No Google Docs support is claimed.
No DOCX support expansion is claimed.
No review import completion is claimed.
No manuscript write is performed by release claim command execution witness binding.
No project truth write is performed by release claim command execution witness binding.
No receipt or recovery evidence is created by release claim command execution witness binding.
No DOCX import safe-create behavior is changed.
No DOCX export behavior is changed.
No import/export MVP closeout is widened.
No PDF, EPUB, HTML, Mindmap, cloud sync, cross-platform readiness, packaged readiness, or Y9 admission is claimed.
layerDecisions: Command execution witness binding is witness-only chain evidence.
Command execution witness binding does not execute release commands.
Command execution witness binding does not prove command execution.
Command execution witness binding does not admit command availability.
Command execution witness binding does not admit release execution completion.
Command execution witness binding does not publish anything.
Command execution witness binding does not become project truth by itself.
The outer command bridge contract still reports non-ok witness through a nested failure envelope instead of a top-level raw witness response.
12L command surface admission remains upstream evidence and is not widened into command execution by this contour.
12K execution gate remains upstream evidence and is not reopened semantically by this contour.
Real command execution remains a separate future owner-approved contour.
USER_FACING_CLAIM_READY remains an internal chain class only, not a release readiness state.
USER_FACING is a chain surface in this contour, not a user-facing UI state.
This contour is not a real release execution contour.
This contour is not a product publication contour.

machineChecks.command: node --test donor port command surface kernel contracts
node --test 12M command execution witness wiring contracts
node --test 12M command execution witness binding contracts
node --test revision bridge release claim contracts

agentAudits.finding: Confirmed the shipped 12L path ends at admission-only command surface wiring and does not create a separate runtime execute surface for admitted summary.commandId values.

Confirmed the next move after 12L must remain one explicit owner-approved bounded contour and must not claim command availability, command execution, release execution completion, product publication, release readiness, user-facing release, import/export widening, apply, storage, receipt, recovery, dependency, or Y9.

Confirmed typed-result passthrough stayed bounded to admit plus execute and blocked or diagnostics witness output stayed nested under the outer command bridge failure envelope; the remaining closeout facts were delivery completion and rebinding the full release-claim machine-check count from 221 to the observed 223.

nextStep: select the next owner-approved boundary explicitly; real command execution, real product publication, and post-MVP import/export expansion do not open by implication
